column1: # Regex benchmark cases — three-way parity (ezi_gex vs Rust `regex` vs Go `regexp`).
#   2. corpora     comma-separated haystack names (no .txt) the case runs on
#   3. pattern     the DEFAULT regex string, fed verbatim to every engine
#   4+. overrides  optional "engine:pattern" (engine in {ezi,go,rust}) for the rare
#                  case where one engine spells the SAME language differently. The
#                  runtime match-count cross-check proves the variants are equivalent.
# Parity rules baked into the corpus mapping (so all three engines agree on the
# non-overlapping match COUNT — the built-in correctness check):
#   * Perl classes \w \d \s and \b are Unicode in Rust/ezi_gex but ASCII in Go
#     (RE2). They are therefore only used on PURE-ASCII corpora (logs; sherlock for
#     \d and \b-of-an-ASCII-literal, both verified to agree). Unicode word scans use
#     \p{L}/\p{N}, which are Unicode in all three.
#   * Bare script names \p{Han}/\p{Cyrillic} are accepted by Go and Rust; ezi_gex
# ── Word-boundary literal (ASCII literal → \b agrees across engines) ──────────
